perturbation.original_text: 1.9 "Net Sales" means, with respect to the Product, the gross invoiced sales price payable to ArTara and/or its Affiliates and their respective licensees and sublicensees for sales anywhere in the world of the Product to a third party, less: (a) discounts (including cash, quantity and patient program discounts), retroactive price reductions, charge-back payments and rebates granted to managed health care organizations or to federal, state and local governments, their agencies, and purchasers and reimbursers or to trade customers; (b) credits or allowances actually, not to exceed the original invoice amount, granted upon claims, damaged goods, rejections or returns of the Product, including the Product returned in connection with recalls or withdrawals; (c) freight out, postage, shipping and insurance charges for delivery of the Product if charged separately and include in the gross receipts; and (d) taxes or duties, excluding income taxes and value-added taxes, levied on, absorbed or otherwise imposed on the sale of the Product, including governmental charges otherwise imposed upon the billed amount, as adjusted for rebates and refunds, provided that such are included in gross receipts and are paid to and/or its Affiliates and their respective licensees and sublicensees. 2 Source: ARTARA THERAPEUTICS, INC., 8-K, 1/10/2020 Net Sales shall be determined in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles, consistently applied.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.9 "Net Sales" means, with respect to the Product, the invoiced sales price payable to ArTara and/or its Affiliates and their respective licensees and sublicensees for sales anywhere in the world of the Product to a third party. Net Sales shall be determined at ArTara's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Net Sales' included a detailed calculation methodology with specific deductions. The modified definition removes these deductions and states that 'Net Sales shall be determined at ArTara's discretion.' This creates an in-text contradiction because Article Three, Sections 3.3 and 3.5, reference 'Net Sales' for royalty and milestone payments, implying a consistent and objective calculation method. ArTara's discretion in defining 'Net Sales' undermines the predictability and enforceability of these payment obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text:
3.3 Royalties. Royalties will be payable by ArTara on Net Sales of Product in the Indication. ArTara will, no later than […***…] following the close of each calendar quarter, pay tiered Royalties based on annual Net Sales of Product in the Indication as set forth below:

Annual Net Sales of Product for the Indication Annual Royalty Rate Percent Net Sales
$0 - $25,000,000 1.75%
>$25,000,000 - $50,000,000 2.25%
>$50,000,000 2.50%
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the detailed calculation methodology for 'Net Sales' and grants ArTara discretion in determining it. This directly contradicts Section 3.3, which specifies royalty payments based on tiered percentages of 'Net Sales'. The original definition provides an objective basis for calculating Net Sales, allowing for a defined Royalty rate. Granting ArTara discretion introduces ambiguity and undermines the enforceability and predictability of royalty payments, creating a direct conflict. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, because essential language was removed creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: 4.3 Right of Reference and IND Assignment. University hereby grants to ArTara an exclusive Right of Reference to all Program Regulatory Filings by University in support of the Product. Upon written request of ArTara, University will assign the IND to ArTara.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.3 Right of Reference and IND Assignment. University hereby grants to ArTara a Right of Reference to all Program Regulatory Filings by University in support of the Product.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause stated that the University would assign the IND to ArTara upon written request. By removing 'Upon written request of ArTara, University will assign the IND to ArTara', Section 8.2, which states 'Upon termination of the Project by ArTara this Agreement will terminate subject to Section 8.3 and ArTara will reassign to University the IND if assignment thereof previously occurred pursuant to Section 4.3' now creates an internal contradiction. As now the the IND cannot be assigned, which affects the reasignment of INDs in case the project is terminated by Artara.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.2 Termination by Either Party. Either Party may terminate the Project and all commitments and obligations with respect thereto, subject to Section 8.3 herein, upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other Party. In the event of any termination of the Project by University, (a) University agrees to complete Phase I and II of the Project, and (b) ArTara will continue to provide annual funding until the completion of Phase II. Upon termination of the Project by ArTara this Agreement will terminate subject to Section 8.3 and ArTara will reassign to University the IND if assignment thereof previously occurred pursuant to Section 4.3.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the clause 'Upon written request of ArTara, University will assign the IND to ArTara' from Section 4.3 directly contradicts Section 8.2, which states 'ArTara will reassign to University the IND if assignment thereof previously occurred pursuant to Section 4.3.' Since the assignment of the IND can no longer occur based on the changed text of Section 4.3, the reassignment provision in Section 8.2 becomes unenforceable and contradictory. This creates a clear in-text contradiction that affects the enforceability of the contract, as it introduces uncertainty regarding the reassignment of INDs upon termination of the project.

perturbation.original_text: University and/or Principal Investigator shall provide ArTara with a copy of any proposed Publication for review and comment at least […***…] prior to submission thereof for publication. ArTara shall have […***…], after receipt of said copy to object to such proposed Publication because there is Confidential Information which needs protection. In the event that ArTara makes such objection, University and/or Principal Investigator shall refrain from submitting such Publication for a maximum of […***…] from date of receipt of such objection in order for ArTara to file patent application(s) directed to patentable subject matter contained in the proposed Publication. If in its review, ArTara identifies information it considers to be its Confidential Information, ArTara may require redaction of that Confidential Information; provided, however, that ArTara shall not require removal of information necessary for complete and accurate presentation and interpretation of the Program Data and results. The Publication shall occur within […***…] of the date the Project is closed or terminated, or University and/or Principal Investigator shall be free to publish Program Data and results at that time.
perturbation.changed_text: University and/or Principal Investigator shall provide ArTara with a copy of any proposed Publication for review and comment prior to submission thereof for publication. If in its review, ArTara identifies information it considers to be its Confidential Information, ArTara may require redaction of that Confidential Information; provided, however, that ArTara shall not require removal of information necessary for presentation of results. The Publication shall occur after the Project is closed or terminated, University and/or Principal Investigator shall be free to publish Program Data and results at that time.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provided specific timelines for ArTara's review and objection to publications, and a maximum hold period for patent filing. The modified text removes these timelines, stating only that ArTara will review the publication and can require redaction of Confidential Information. By removing that timeline creates an in-text contradiction when reffering to Article Five, Sections 5.1 and 5.2, which define procedures and exceptions for what is considered confidential information, but no process is given to ArTara for when they should use the confidential information within publication of results.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 Confidentiality. During the term of this Agreement and for a period of seven (7) years after its termination or expiration each Party (the "Receiving Party") shall maintain in confidence and, except as authorized by this Agreement, not use any know-how, data, processes, techniques, formulas, test data and other information disclosed by the other Party (the "Disclosing Party") and which for any of the foregoing, if written, is marked "Confidential" by the Disclosing Party or, if verbal or visual, is identified in writing as "Confidential" at the time of disclosure and reduced to writing by the Disclosing Party within thirty (30) days of the verbal or visual disclosure ("Confidential Information").
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes the specific timelines for ArTara's review, objection, and patent filing, which directly contradicts the expectation of defined procedures for confidential information handling as outlined in Article Five, Sections 5.1. Section 5.1 states that each party will maintain confidence during the term of the agreement. Without a timeline, this becomes an obligation without a procedure, leading to a contradiction. The omission of these timelines makes the process for handling confidential information during publication ambiguous and potentially unenforceable, thus creating a direct conflict.
